time.Minute,\n\t\tCanarySize: 1024,\n\t\tMaxRetries: 3,\n\t\tRetryDelay: 10 * time.Second,\n\t})\n\n\t// Create provenance manager\n\tprovenanceMgr := provenance.NewManager(b2Backend, cfg.Bucket, cfg.WriterID)\n\n\t// Create logger\n\tlogger := logging.New(\"armor\")\n\n\t// Create presign signer\n\tpresigner := presign.NewSigner(cfg.PresignSecret, cfg.PresignBaseURL)\n\n\t// Create B2 keys client (for B2 native API key management)\n\t// Uses the same B2 credentials as the S3 backend\n\tvar b2keysClient *b2keys.Client\n\tif cfg.Bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t// Bucket-scoped configuration: application key is restricted to a single bucket\n\t\t// and cannot call account-level B2 API endpoints (like b2_authorize_account).\n\t\t// Key management is unavailable in this mode, which is expected and intentional.\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"B2 key management disabled (application key is bucket-scoped)\")\n\t} else {\n\t\t// No bucket restriction: attempt to create B2 keys client for account-level operations\n\t\t// Extract account ID from the key ID (B2 key IDs are in format accountIdKeyId)\n\t\t// The account ID is the first 12 characters\n\t\taccountID := cfg.B2AccessKeyID\n\t\tif len(accountID) > 12 {\n\t\t\taccountID = accountID[:12]\n\t\t}\n\t\tb2keysClient, err = b2keys.NewClient(context.Background(), accountID, cfg.B2SecretAccessKey)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Warn(\"Failed to create B2 keys client - key management disabled\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create dashboard with optional authentication\n\tdash := dashboard.NewWithAuth(b2Backend, cfg.Bucket, metrics.DefaultMetrics,\n\t\tcfg.DashboardUser, cfg.DashboardPass, cfg.DashboardToken)\n\n\t// Load manifest index from B2 (startup load).\n\t// The manifest is a performance optimisation — errors are logged as warnings\n\t// and do not prevent startup.\n\tvar manifestIdx *manifest.Index\n\tif cfg.ManifestEnabled {\n\t\tmanifestIdx = manifest.New()\n\t\t// ListRaw bypasses the .armor/ filter so manifest keys are discoverable.\n\t\tlister := func(ctx context.Context, prefix, token string) ([]string, string, error) {\n\t\t\tresult, lerr := b2Backend.ListRaw(ctx, cfg.Bucket, prefix, \"\", token, 1000)\n\t\t\tif lerr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, \"\", lerr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(result.Objects))\n\t\t\tfor i, obj := range result.Objects {\n\t\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn keys, result.NextToken, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Fetch snapshot and delta blobs via Cloudflare for free egress and edge\n\t\t// caching. Fall back to direct B2 if no Cloudflare domain is configured.\n\t\tfetcher := func(ctx context.Context, key string) ([]byte, error) {\n\t\t\tvar body io.ReadCloser\n\t\t\tvar ferr error\n\t\t\tif cfg.CFDomain != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.Get(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.GetDirect(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif ferr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, ferr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n\t\t\treturn io.ReadAll(body)\n\t\t}\n\t\tloadStart := time.Now()\n\t\tif loadErr := manifest.Load(context.Background(), manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, lister, fetcher); loadErr != nil {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": loadErr.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Warn(\"manifest startup load failed — continuing with empty manifest index\")\n\t\t\tmanifestIdx = manifest.New() // reset to empty on error\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"entries\":  manifestIdx.Len(),\n\t\t\t\t\"seq\":      manifestIdx.Seq(),\n\t\t\t\t\"duration\": time.Since(loadStart).String(),\n\t\t\t}).Info(\"manifest index loaded\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create and start the manifest writer (async delta persistence to B2).\n\t// Uploads go direct to B2 (free ingress); no Cloudflare path needed here.\n\tvar manifestWriter *manifest.Writer\n\tvar manifestCompactor *manifest.Compactor\n\tif manifestIdx != nil {\n\t\tuploader := func(ctx context.Context, key string, data []byte) error {\n\t\t\treturn b2Backend.Put(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(data), int64(len(data)), nil)\n\t\t}\n\t\tmanifestWriter = manifest.NewWriter(manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, uploader, 0)\n\n\t\t// Create compactor that lists and batch-deletes via the B2 backend.\n\t\tlisterForCompactor := func(ctx context.Context, prefix, token string) ([]string, string, error) {\n\t\t\tresult, lerr := b2Backend.ListRaw(ctx, cfg.Bucket, prefix, \"\", token, 1000)\n\t\t\tif lerr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, \"\", lerr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(result.Objects))\n\t\t\tfor i, obj := range result.Objects {\n\t\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn keys, result.NextToken, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tdeleter := func(ctx context.Context, keys []string) error {\n\t\t\treturn b2Backend.DeleteObjects(ctx, cfg.Bucket, keys)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcompactionInterval := time.Duration(cfg.ManifestCompactionInterval) * time.Second\n\t\tmanifestCompactor = manifest.NewCompactor(\n\t\t\tmanifestIdx,\n\t\t\tcfg.ManifestPrefix,\n\t\t\tcfg.WriterID,\n\t\t\tuploader,\n\t\t\tlisterForCompactor,\n\t\t\tdeleter,\n\t\t\tcompactionInterval,\n\t\t\tcfg.ManifestCompactionThreshold,\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// Wire writer → compactor: after each delta flush, notify compactor.\n\t\tmanifestWriter.SetOnFlush(manifestCompactor.NotifyDelta)\n\n\t\tmanifestWriter.Start(context.Background())\n\t\tmanifestCompactor.Start(context.Background())\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"manifest writer and compactor started\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Server{\n\t\tconfig:         cfg,\n\t\tbackend:        b2Backend,\n\t\tcache:          cache,\n\t\tfooterCache:    footerCache,\n\t\tlistCache:      listCache,\n\t\tkeyManager:     keyMgr,\n\t\tcanary:         canaryMonitor,\n\t\tcanaryDisabled: cfg.CanaryDisabled,\n\t\tprovenance:     provenanceMgr,\n\t\tpresigner:      presigner,\n\t\tb2keys:         b2keysClient,\n\t\tdashboard:      dash,\n\t\tmanifest:          manifestIdx,\n\t\tmanifestWriter:    manifestWriter,\n\t\tmanifestCompactor: manifestCompactor,\n\t\tmetrics:        metrics.DefaultMetrics,\n\t\trequestTracker: metrics.DefaultRequestTracker,\n\t\tlogger:         logger,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// manifestRecorder implements handlers.ManifestRecorder. It updates the\n// in-memory index synchronously (so subsequent HeadObject calls see the new\n// state immediately) and enqueues the op for async B2 delta persistence.\ntype manifestRecorder struct {\n\tidx    *manifest.Index\n\twriter *manifest.Writer\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string) {\n\tentry := &manifest.Entry{\n\t\tPlaintextSize:   size,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA256: sha256Hex,\n\t\tIV:              iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:      wrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:       blockSize,\n\t\tContentType:     contentType,\n\t\tETag:            etag,\n\t\tLastModified:    time.Now().UTC(),\n\t}\n\tm.idx.Put(bucket, key, entry)\n\tm.writer.EnqueuePut(bucket, key, entry)\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordDelete(bucket, key string) {\n\tm.idx.Delete(bucket, key)\n\tm.writer.EnqueueDelete(bucket, key)\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) Lookup(bucket, key string) (*handlers.ManifestEntry, bool) {\n\tentry, ok := m.idx.Get(bucket, key)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, false\n\t}\n\treturn &handlers.ManifestEntry{\n\t\tPlaintextSize: entry.PlaintextSize,\n\t\tContentType:   entry.ContentType,\n\t\tETag:          entry.ETag,\n\t\tLastModified:  entry.LastModified,\n\t\tIV:            entry.IV,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    entry.WrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:     entry.BlockSize,\n\t}, true\n}\n\n// StopManifestWriter flushes any pending manifest ops and stops the async writer.\nfunc (s *Server) StopManifestWriter() {\n\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\ts.manifestWriter.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest writer stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// StopManifestCompactor stops the background compaction goroutine.\nfunc (s *Server) StopManifestCompactor() {\n\tif s.manifestCompactor != nil {\n\t\ts.manifestCompactor.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest compactor stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// StartCanary starts the canary monitor.\n// It should be called after the server is created.\n// When ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true, the monitor is not started and /readyz\n// always returns 200 regardless of B2 reachability.\nfunc (s *Server) StartCanary(ctx context.Context) {\n\tif s.canary == nil || s.canaryStarted {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true)\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.canaryStarted = true\n\ts.canary.Start(ctx)\n\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor started\")\n}\n\n// StopCanary stops the canary monitor.\nfunc (s *Server) StopCanary() {\n\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n\t\ts.canary.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// Handler returns the main S3 API handler.\nfunc (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {\n\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n\n\t// Health endpoints\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/readyz\", s.readyz)\n\n\t// Share endpoint for pre-signed URLs (public, no auth required)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/share/\", s.handleShare)\n\n\t// S3 operations\n\th := handlers.New(s.config, s.backend, s.cache, s.footerCache, s.keyManager, s.listCache)\n\n\t// Wire up provenance if available\n\tif s.provenance != nil {\n\t\th.WithProvenance(s.provenance)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wire up manifest recorder if enabled\n\tif s.manifest != nil && s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\th.WithManifest(&manifestRecorder{idx: s.manifest, writer: s.manifestWriter})\n\t}\n\n\t// Bucket operations\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/\", s.wrapHandler(h.HandleRoot))\n\n\treturn mux\n}\n\n// AdminHandler returns the admin API handler.\nfunc (s *Server) AdminHandler() http.Handler {\n\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/verify\", s.verifyKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/rotate\", s.rotateKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/export\", s.exportKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/canary\", s.canaryHandler)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/audit\", s.audit)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/presign\", s.handlePresign)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys\", s.handleB2Keys)           // GET=List, POST=Create\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys/\", s.handleB2KeyDelete)     // DELETE=Delete by ID\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/metrics\", s.metrics.Handler())\n\n\t// Dashboard routes (authenticated via DashboardUser/Pass or DashboardToken)\n\tif s.dashboard != nil {\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth()) // For prefix navigation\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/object\", s.dashboard.ObjectDetailHandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/metrics\", s.dashboard.MetricsHandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/encryption-stats\", s.dashboard.EncryptionStatsHandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/api/list\", s.dashboard.ListAPIHandlerWithAuth())\n\n\t\t// Key rotation proxy handler (authenticated)\n\t\tadminClient := &http.Client{\n\t\t\tTimeout: 30 * time.Minute, // Key rotation can take a long time\n\t\t}\n\t\tadminURL := \"http://\" + s.config.AdminListen + \"/admin/key/rotate\"\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/rotate\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateHandlerWithAuth(adminClient, adminURL))\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/status\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateStatusHandlerWithAuth())\n\t}\n\n\treturn mux\n}\n\n// healthz returns the health status.\n// This is the liveness probe — it only checks that the process is alive.\n// Backend connectivity is checked by /readyz (the readiness probe).\nfunc (s *Server) healthz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n}\n\n// readyz returns the readiness status.\n// When the canary monitor is running and not disabled, its in-memory health\n// state is the sole signal — no backend call is made. When the canary is\n// disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true), /readyz always returns 200 and the\n// liveness probe (/healthz) is the sole health guard. When the canary is not\n// configured, the manifest writer's last flush is used as the health signal.\nfunc (s *Server) readyz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t// When canary is explicitly disabled, skip all backend checks.\n\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Canary monitor is authoritative when running.\n\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n\t\tif !s.canary.IsHealthy() {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - canary check failed\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback: manifest writer's last successful delta flush.\n\t// A flush within the last 60 seconds indicates the service is healthy.\n\tconst flushThreshold = 60 * time.Second\n\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\tlastFlush := s.manifestWriter.LastFlush()\n\t\tif !lastFlush.IsZero() && time.Since(lastFlush) < flushThreshold {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// No recent flush — report unhealthy\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\t\tif lastFlush.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - manifest writer has never flushed\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, \"Not ready - manifest writer last flush %v ago (threshold %v)\", time.Since(lastFlush).Round(time.Second), flushThreshold)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Neither canary nor manifest writer available — report unhealthy.\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - no health signal available\"))\n}\n\n// verifyKey verifies the MEK is correct.\nfunc (s *Server) verifyKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif s.canary == nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n\tif status.DecryptVerified && status.HMACVerified {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"verified\",\"message\":\"MEK is correct\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unverified\",\"error\":\"canary check failed - MEK may be incorrect\"}`))\n}\n\n// rotateKey rotates the MEK.\nfunc (s *Server) rotateKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Read the new MEK from the request body\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer r.Body.Close()\n\n\t// The new MEK should be a 32-byte hex-encoded string (64 hex chars)\n\t// or raw 32 bytes\n\tvar newMEK []byte\n\tif len(body) == 64 {\n\t\t// Hex-encoded\n\t\tnewMEK, err = hex.DecodeString(string(body))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid hex-encoded MEK\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t} else if len(body) == 32 {\n\t\t// Raw bytes\n\t\tnewMEK = body\n\t} else {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid MEK length: expected 32 bytes or 64 hex chars, got %d\", len(body)), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create key rotator with the default key. Pass the manifest index so\n\t// rotateObject can skip per-object HeadObject calls when the entry is cached.\n\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n\trotator := NewKeyRotator(s.backend, s.config.Bucket, defaultKey.MEK, newMEK, s.manifest)\n\n\t// Perform rotation\n\tresult, err := rotator.Rotate(r.Context())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"status\":  \"failed\",\n\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n\t\t\t\"result\":  result,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update the server's MEK on success\n\tif result.Status == \"completed\" {\n\t\tif err := s.keyManager.UpdateDefaultKey(newMEK); err != nil {\n\t\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Error(\"failed to update key manager after rotation\")\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Clear the metadata cache since DEKs are now wrapped with new MEK\n\t\ts.cache.Clear()\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n}\n\n// exportKey exports the current MEK.\nfunc (s *Server) exportKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"confirm\") != \"yes\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Must include ?confirm=yes to export key\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Export the default MEK as hex-encoded string\n\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{\n\t\t\"mek\":   hex.EncodeToString(defaultKey.MEK),\n\t\t\"format\": \"hex\",\n\t\t\"warning\": \"This key provides access to all encrypted data. Store securely.\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// canaryHandler returns the canary status.\nfunc (s *Server) canaryHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif s.canary == nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(status)\n}\n\n// audit returns the audit status.\nfunc (s *Server) audit(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create auditor and perform audit\n\tauditor := provenance.NewAuditor(s.backend, s.config.Bucket)\n\tresult, err := auditor.Audit(r.Context())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"status\": \"error\",\n\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n}\n\n// wrapHandler wraps a handler with common middleware.\nfunc (s *Server) wrapHandler(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tstart := time.Now()\n\n\t\t// Track in-flight request\n\t\ts.requestTracker.Start()\n\t\tdefer s.requestTracker.End()\n\n\t\t// Log request\n\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"method\": r.Method,\n\t\t\t\"path\":   r.URL.Path,\n\t\t}).Debug(\"incoming request\")\n\n\t\t// Add CORS headers for browser clients\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", \"*\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n\n\t\t// Handle preflight\n\t\tif r.Method == http.MethodOptions {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify auth for non-public endpoints\n\t\tif !s.isPublicPath(r.URL.Path) {\n\t\t\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif authErr, ok := err.(*AuthError); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, authErr.Code, authErr.Message, 403)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"auth\", 403)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check ACL for the request. For list operations the URL path has no\n\t\t\t// key component, so fall back to the ?prefix query param so that ACL\n\t\t\t// prefix restrictions are enforced correctly against the listed prefix.\n\t\t\tbucket, key := s.extractBucketAndKey(r)\n\t\t\tif key == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tkey = r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, key); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"acl\", 403)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Decode aws-chunked body when MinIO streaming signature is used.\n\t\t// The seed signature in the Authorization header already authenticated the\n\t\t// request; we only need to strip the chunk framing to recover the payload.\n\t\tif r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\") == \"STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD\" {\n\t\t\tif decoded := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Decoded-Content-Length\"); decoded != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.ParseInt(decoded, 10, 64); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tr.ContentLength = n\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tr.Body = newAWSChunkedReader(r.Body)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Use a response writer wrapper to capture status code\n\t\trw := &responseWriter{ResponseWriter: w, statusCode: http.StatusOK}\n\n\t\th(rw, r)\n\n\t\t// Record metrics\n\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(r.Method, rw.statusCode)\n\t\ts.metrics.RecordRequestDuration(r.Method, duration)\n\n\t\t// Log completed request\n\t\tfields := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"method\":      r.Method,\n\t\t\t\"path\":        r.URL.Path,\n\t\t\t\"status\":      rw.statusCode,\n\t\t\t\"duration_ms\": duration.Milliseconds(),\n\t\t}\n\t\tif rng := r.Header.Get(\"Range\"); rng != \"\" {\n\t\t\tfields[\"range\"] = rng\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.logger.WithFields(fields).Info(\"request completed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// responseWriter wraps http.ResponseWriter to capture the status code.\ntype responseWriter struct {\n\thttp.ResponseWriter\n\tstatusCode int\n}\n\n// WriteHeader captures the status code.\nfunc (rw *responseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {\n\trw.statusCode = code\n\trw.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)\n}\n\n// isPublicPath checks if a path is public (no auth required).\nfunc (s *Server) isPublicPath(path string) bool {\n\treturn path == \"/healthz\" || path == \"/readyz\"\n}\n\n// verifyAuthAndGetCredential validates AWS SigV4 authentication and returns the credential.\nfunc (s *Server) verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\t// Create auth with all credentials\n\tauth := NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(s.config.Credentials, s.config.B2Region)\n\n\t// Check for query-based auth (presigned URLs)\n\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\") != \"\" {\n\t\treturn auth.VerifyQueryAuth(r)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for header-based auth\n\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Pass nil body — buildCanonicalRequest uses x-amz-content-sha256 header\n\t// when present (which covers all AWS SDK/CLI requests), falling back to\n\t// sha256(body) for requests without that header.\n\treturn auth.VerifyRequest(r, nil)\n}\n\n// extractBucketAndKey extracts bucket and key from the request URL.\nfunc (s *Server) extractBucketAndKey(r *http.Request) (bucket, key string) {\n\tpath := r.URL.Path\n\t// Remove leading slash\n\tpath = strings.TrimPrefix(path, \"/\")\n\n\t// Check for virtual-hosted-style (bucket in host)\n\t// For path-style: /bucket/key\n\tparts := strings.SplitN(path, \"/\", 2)\n\tif len(parts) >= 1 {\n\t\tbucket = parts[0]\n\t}\n\tif len(parts) >= 2 {\n\t\tkey = parts[1]\n\t\t// URL decode the key (DuckDB httpfs encodes special chars like = as %3D)\n\t\tif decoded, err := url.PathUnescape(key); err == nil {\n\t\t\tkey = decoded\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use configured bucket if empty\n\tif bucket == \"\" {\n\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n\t}\n\n\treturn bucket, key\n}\n\n// writeError writes an S3 error response.\nfunc (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n\tvar codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n\txml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n\txml.EscapeText(&msgBuf, []byte(message))\n\tfmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`+\"\\n<Error>\\n  <Code>%s</Code>\\n  <Message>%s</Message>\\n</Error>\",\n\t\tcodeBuf.String(), msgBuf.String())\n}\n\n// GenerateDEK generates a new DEK (exposed for handlers).\nfunc (s *Server) GenerateDEK() ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn crypto.GenerateDEK()\n}\n\n// GenerateIV generates a new IV (exposed for handlers).\nfunc (s *Server) GenerateIV() ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn crypto.GenerateIV()\n}\n\n// WaitForInFlightRequests waits for all in-flight requests to complete.\nfunc (s *Server) WaitForInFlightRequests() {\n\ts.requestTracker.Wait()\n}\n\n// InFlightRequestCount returns the current number of in-flight requests.\nfunc (s *Server) InFlightRequestCount() int64 {\n\treturn s.requestTracker.Count()\n}\n\n// handlePresign generates a pre-signed URL for sharing encrypted files.\n// POST /admin/presign\n// Body: {\"bucket\": \"my-bucket\", \"key\": \"path/to/file.parquet\", \"expires_in\": \"1h\", \"content_disposition\": \"attachment; filename=\\\"file.parquet\\\"\"}\nfunc (s *Server) handlePresign(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify auth\n\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Return specific authentication error code and message\n\t\tif authErr, ok := err.(*AuthError); ok {\n\t\t\ts.writeError(w, authErr.Code, authErr.Message, 403)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse request body\n\tvar req struct {\n\t\tBucket             string `json:\"bucket\"`\n\t\tKey                string `json:\"key\"`\n\t\tExpiresIn          string `json:\"expires_in\"`\n\t\tContentDisposition string `json:\"content_disposition\"`\n\t\tRange              string `json:\"range\"`\n\t}\n\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Use configured bucket if not specified\n\tbucket := req.Bucket\n\tif bucket == \"\" {\n\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate required fields\n\tif req.Key == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"key is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check ACL for the request\n\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, req.Key); err != nil {\n\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse expiration (default 1 hour)\n\texpiration := presign.DefaultExpiration\n\tif req.ExpiresIn != \"\" {\n\t\texpiration, err = presign.ParseExpiration(req.ExpiresIn)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid expires_in: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build options\n\tvar opts []presign.Option\n\tif req.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithContentDisposition(req.ContentDisposition))\n\t}\n\tif req.Range != \"\" {\n\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithRange(req.Range))\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate pre-signed URL\n\tshareURL, err := s.presigner.GenerateURL(bucket, req.Key, expiration, opts...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate URL: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Return response\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"url\":       shareURL,\n\t\t\"expires_in\": presign.FormatExpiration(expiration),\n\t\t\"expires_at\": time.Now().Add(expiration).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),\n\t})\n}\n\n// handleShare serves decrypted content from a pre-signed URL token.\n// GET /share/<token>\nfunc (s *Server) handleShare(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract token from path\n\ttokenStr := presign.ParseTokenFromPath(r.URL.Path)\n\tif tokenStr == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Missing token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify token\n\ttoken, err := s.presigner.VerifyToken(tokenStr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrExpiredToken) {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Link expired\", http.StatusGone)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrInvalidSignature) {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid link\", http.StatusForbidden)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get object metadata\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tinfo, err := s.backend.Head(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if object is ARMOR-encrypted\n\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\t// Serve non-ARMOR objects directly (passthrough)\n\t\tbody, _, err := s.backend.Get(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer body.Close()\n\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", info.Size))\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n\t\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to parse object metadata\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the MEK for this object\n\tmek, err := s.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to get decryption key\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to unwrap DEK\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to create decryptor\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set response headers\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", armorMeta.PlaintextSize))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle range request if specified in token or header\n\trangeHeader := token.Range\n\tif rangeHeader == \"\" {\n\t\trangeHeader = r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n\t}\n\n\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n\t\ts.handleShareRangeRequest(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta, rangeHeader)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Full object download\n\ts.handleShareFullObject(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta)\n}\n\n// handleShareFullObject handles full object downloads for share endpoint.\nfunc (s *Server) handleShareFullObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(armorMeta.PlaintextSize, blockSize))\n\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n\n\t// Calculate offsets\n\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n\n\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table\n\thmacBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to fetch HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\thmacTable, err := io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\thmacBody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// 2. Stream data from Cloudflare\n\tstreamSize := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n\tdataBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, 0, streamSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n\n\t// 3. Read and discard header\n\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// 4. Write status before streaming\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\n\t// 5. Stream decrypt\n\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n\n\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif n == 0 {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n\n\t\t// Verify HMAC\n\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n\n\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n\n\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Decrypt\n\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n\n\t\t// Write to client\n\t\tw.Write(decrypted)\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleShareRangeRequest handles range requests for share endpoint.\nfunc (s *Server) handleShareRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, rangeHeader string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n\n\t// Parse range header\n\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid range\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, armorMeta.BlockSize, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to translate range: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel\n\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n\n\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n\n\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\tencryptedBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(gctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n\n\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n\n\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\thmacBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(gctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n\n\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n\n\tif err := g.Wait(); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Decrypt range\n\tplaintext, err := decryptor.DecryptRange(encrypted, hmacTable, start, end, plaintextSize, false)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decrypt range: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set response headers\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", len(plaintext)))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n\tw.Write(plaintext)\n}\n\nfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\nfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n\treturn counter\n}\n\n// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\nfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n\n\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t} else {\n\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start || end >= totalSize {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn start, end, nil\n}\n\n// handleB2Keys handles B2 application key management.\n// GET: List keys\n// POST: Create a new key\nfunc (s *Server) handleB2Keys(w (m *mockBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n245\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n246\t\tdefer uploadID)\n257\t\t}\n258\t\tm.multipartData[uploadID][partNumber] = data\n259\t\n260\t\t// Return ETag (use hash of data for uniqueness)\n261\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x-%d\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\n262\t}\n263\t\n264\tfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n265\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n266\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n267\t\n268\t\t// Check if upload exists\n269\t\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n270\t\tif !exists {\n271\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n272\t\t}\n273\t\n274\t\t// Combine all parts in order\n275\t\tvar combined []byte\n276\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n277\t\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n278\t\t\tif !ok {\n279\t\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n280\t\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n281\t\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n282\t\t\t}\n283\t\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\t// Store the combined object\n287\t\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n288\t\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n289\t\n290\t\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n291\t\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n292\t\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n293\t\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n294\t\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n295\t\t}\n296\t\n297\t\t// Clean up multipart data\n298\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n299\t\n300\t\t// Return final ETag\n301\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\tfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx {\n535\t\tmb := newMockBackend()\n536\t\n537\t\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n538\t\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n539\t\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n540\t\n541\t\twrongMEK := make([]byte, := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n550\t\tif err != nil {\n551\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n552\t\t}\n553\t\n554\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n555\t\tif err != nil {\n556\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n557\t\t}\n558\t\n559\t\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n560\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n561\t\tif err != nil nil {\n580\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n581\t\t}\n582\t\n583\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n584\t\tif err != nil {\n585\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n586\t\t}\n587\t\n588\t\t// Build envelope\n589\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n590\t\tenvelope = wrappedDEK,\n605\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n606\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n607\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n608\t\n609\t\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n610\t\tkey nil {\n617\t\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n618\t\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n619\t\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := {\n\treturn m.Get(ctx, bucket, key)\n}\n\n// Multipart upload methods (functional implementations for testing)\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tuploadID := fmt.Sprintf(\"upload-%d\", time.Now().UnixNano())\n\t// Initialize storage for this upload's parts\n\tm.multipartData[uploadID] = make(map[int32][]byte)\n\t// Store metadata for later use when completing\n\tm.meta[bucket+\"/\"+key+\"__pending__\"+uploadID] = meta\n\treturn uploadID, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Read the part data\n\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read part data: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the part data\n\tif _, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]; !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tm.multipartData[uploadID][partNumber] = data\n\n\t// Return ETag (use hash of data for uniqueness)\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x-%d\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check if upload exists\n\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine all parts in order\n\tvar combined []byte\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the combined object\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n\n\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Return final ETag\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Clean up pending metadata\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\nfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\n\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n\n\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n\n\t// Create test content\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n\n\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\tif verifyErr == nil {\n\t\t\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\n\t\t\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\t\tif decryptErr == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\n\t\tt.Logf(\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\nfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tInterval:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Fast for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Start the monitor\n\tm.Start(ctx)\n\n\t// Wait for at least one check\n\ttime.Sleep(150 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Should be healthy now\n\tif !m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected monitor to be uploading a known-content\n// canary file, downloading it through Cloudflare, and verifying the decryption.\npackage canary\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/rand\"\n\t\"encoding/binary\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/backend\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/crypto\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/metrics\"\n)\n\n// Status represents the health status of the canary.\ntype Status string\n\nconst (\n\tStatusHealthy   Status = \"healthy\"\n\tStatusUnhealthy Status = \"unhealthy\"\n\tStatusUnknown   Status = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// CanaryState holds the current state of the canary monitor.\ntype CanaryState struct {\n\tmu sync.RWMutex\n\n\tStatus              Status    `json:\"status\"`\n\tLastCheck `json:\"status\"`\n\tLastCheck         time.Time `json:\"last_check\"`\n\tUploadLatencyMs   int64     `json:\"upload_latency_ms\"`\n\tDownloadLatencyMs int64     `json:\"download_latency_ms\"`\n\tDecryptVerified   bool      `json:\"decrypt_verified\"`\n\tHMACVerified      bool      `json:\"hmac_verified\"`\n\tCFCacheHit        bool `json:\"multipart_healthy\"`\n\tMultipartLastCheck        time.Time `json:\"multipart_last_check\"`\n\tMultipartConsecutiveFails int       `json:\"multipart_consecutive_fails\"`\n\tMultipartLastError        string    `json:\"multipart_last_error,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Monitor manages the canary integrity checks.\ntype Monitor struct {\n\tbackend    backend.Backend\n\tbucket     string\n\tmek        []byte\n\tblockSize  int\n\tinstanceID string\n\n\tstate CanaryState\n\n\t// Configuration\n\tinterval          time.Duration\n\tcanarySize        int\n\tmaxRetries        int\n\tretryDelay        time.Duration\n\tmultipartInterval time.Duration\n\tmultipartSize     int\n\n\t// Control\n\tstopCh chan struct{}\n\tdoneCh chan struct{}\n}\n\n// Config holds configuration for the canary monitor.\ntype Config struct {\n\tBackend          backend.Backend\n\tBucket           string\n\tMEK              []byte\n\tBlockSize        int\n\tInstanceID       string\n\tInterval         time.Duration // Check interval (default 5 minutes)\n\tCanarySize       int           // Size of canary content (default 1024 bytes)\n\tMaxRetries       int           // Max retries on failure (default 3)\n\tRetryDelay       time.Duration // Delay between retries (default 10s)\n\tMultipartInterval time.Duration // Multipart check interval (default 1 hour)\n\tMultipartSize     int           // Size of multipart canary (default 6MB)\n}\n\n// NewMonitor creates a new canary monitor.\nfunc NewMonitor(cfg Config) *Monitor {\n\tif cfg.Interval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.Interval = 5 * time.Minute\n\t}\n\tif cfg.CanarySize == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.CanarySize = 1024\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MaxRetries == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MaxRetries = 3\n\t}\n\tif cfg.RetryDelay == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.RetryDelay = 10 * time.Second\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartInterval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartInterval = 1 * time.Hour\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartSize == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartSize = 6 * 1024 * 1024 // 6MB (above 5MB threshold)\n\t}\n\n\tinstanceID := cfg.InstanceID\n\tif instanceID == \"\" {\n\t\tinstanceID, _ = os.Hostname()\n\t\tif instanceID == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Generate random ID\n\t\t\tb := make([]byte, 8)\n\t\t\trand.Read(b)\n\t\t\tinstanceID = hex.EncodeToString(b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Monitor{\n\t\tbackend:           cfg.Backend,\n\t\tbucket:            cfg.Bucket,\n\t\tmek:               cfg.MEK,\n\t\tblockSize:         cfg.BlockSize,\n\t\tinstanceID:        instanceID,\n\t\tinterval:          cfg.Interval,\n\t\tcanarySize:        cfg.CanarySize,\n\t\tmaxRetries:        cfg.MaxRetries,\n\t\tretryDelay:        cfg.RetryDelay,\n\t\tmultipartInterval: cfg.MultipartInterval,\n\t\tmultipartSize:     cfg.MultipartSize,\n\t\tstopCh:            make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tdoneCh:            make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tstate: CanaryState{\n\t\t\tStatus:           StatusUnknown,\n\t\t\tMultipartHealthy: StatusUnknown,\n\t\t\tInstanceID:       instanceID,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// Start begins the periodic canary checks.\n// It runs an initial check immediately, then periodically.\nfunc (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) {\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer close(m.doneCh)\n\n\t\t// Initial checks\n\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\t\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\n\n\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)\n\t\tmultipartTicker := time.NewTicker(m.multipartInterval)\n\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\t\tdefer multipartTicker.Stop()\n\n\t\tfor {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-m.stopCh:\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\tcase <-multipartTicker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n}\n\n// Stop stops the canary monitor.\nfunc (m *Monitor) Stop() {\n\tclose(m.stopCh)\n\t<-m.doneCh\n}\n\n// runCheck performs a single canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tm.updateStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// runMultipartCheck performs a single multipart canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runMultipartCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.checkMultipart(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateMultipartStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(true)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(false)\n\tm.updateMultipartStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// check performs a single canary integrity check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) check(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.canarySize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload to B2 (direct, not through Cloudflare)\n\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n\tif err := m.backend.Put(ctx, m.bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Download through Cloudflare (via GetRangeWithHeaders to capture CF-Cache-Status)\n\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tbody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\n\t// CF-Cache-Status values: HIT, MISS, EXPIRED, STALE, BYPASS, REVALIDATED, UPDATING, IGNORED\n\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\"CF-Cache-Status\"]; ok {\n\t\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \"HIT\" || cfStatus == \"STALE\" || cfStatus == \"REVALIDATED\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse header\n\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\n\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\n\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\n\n\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\")\n\t}\n\n\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\n\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create decryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify HMACs\n\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\n\t\tresult.HMACVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.HMACVerified = true\n\n\t// Decrypt\n\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\n\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"decrypted content does not match original\")\n\t}\n\tresult.DecryptVerified = true\n\n\t// Verify plaintext SHA\n\tif err := downloadedHeader.VerifyPlaintextSHA(decrypted); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tcleanupCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t\tm.backend.Delete(cleanupCtx, m.bucket, key)\n\t}()\n\n\tresult.Status = StatusHealthy\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// checkMultipart performs a multipart canary check to exercise the multipart upload path.\nfunc (m *Monitor) checkMultipart(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.multipartSize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate multipart canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary-multipart/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload via multipart API (exercises create/upload/complete path)\n\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n\n\t// Step 1: Create multipart upload\n\tuploadID, err := m.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Upload parts (split into 2MB parts for reasonable part count)\n\tconst partSize = 2 * 1024 * 1024 // 2MB parts\n\tvar parts []backend.CompletedPart\n\tpartNum := int32(1)\n\n\tfor offset := 0; offset < len(envelope); offset += partSize {\n\t\tend := offset + partSize\n\t\tif end > len(envelope) {\n\t\t\tend = len(envelope)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartData := envelope[offset:end]\n\t\tpartETag, err := m.backend.UploadPart(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, partNum, bytes.NewReader(partData), int64(len(partData)))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// Abort on failure\n\t\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload part %d: %w\", partNum, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tparts = append(parts, backend.CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: partNum,\n\t\t\tETag:       partETag,\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tpartNum++\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 3: Complete multipart upload\n\t_, err = m.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Abort on failure\n\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to complete multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = io.ReadAll(body)\n\tbody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded multipart canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = `json:\"multipart_last_error,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Monitor manages the canary integrity checks.\ntype Monitor struct {\n\tbackend    backend.Backend\n\tbucket     string\n\tmek // Size of multipart canary (default 6MB)\n}\n\n// NewMonitor creates a new canary monitor.\nfunc NewMonitor(cfg Config) *Monitor {\n\tif cfg.Interval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.Interval = 5 * time.Second\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartInterval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartInterval = 1 * time.Hour\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartSize == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartSize = 6 * 1024 * 1024 // 6MB (above 5MB cfg.MEK,\n\t\tblockSize:         cfg.BlockSize,\n\t\tinstanceID:        instanceID,\n\t\tinterval:          cfg.Interval,\n\t\tcanarySize:        cfg.CanarySize,\n\t\tmaxRetries:
