time.NewTicker(m.multipartInterval)\n\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\t\tdefer multipartTicker.Stop()\n\n\t\tfor {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-m.stopCh:\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\tcase <-multipartTicker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n}\n\n// Stop stops the canary monitor.\nfunc (m *Monitor) Stop() {\n\tclose(m.stopCh)\n\t<-m.doneCh\n}\n\n// runCheck performs a single canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tm.updateStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// runMultipartCheck performs a single multipart canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runMultipartCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.checkMultipart(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateMultipartStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(true)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(false)\n\tm.updateMultipartStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// check performs a single canary integrity check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) check(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.canarySize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload to B2 (direct, not through Cloudflare)\n\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n\tif err := m.backend.Put(ctx, m.bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Download through Cloudflare (via GetRangeWithHeaders to capture CF-Cache-Status)\n\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tbody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\n\t// CF-Cache-Status values: HIT, MISS, EXPIRED, STALE, BYPASS, REVALIDATED, UPDATING, IGNORED\n\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\"CF-Cache-Status\"]; ok {\n\t\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \"HIT\" || cfStatus == \"STALE\" || cfStatus == \"REVALIDATED\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse header\n\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\n\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\n\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\n\n\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\")\n\t}\n\n\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\n\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create decryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify HMACs\n\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\n\t\tresult.HMACVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.HMACVerified = true\n\n\t// Decrypt\n\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\n\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"decrypted content does not match original\")\n\t}\n\tresult.DecryptVerified = true\n\n\t// Verify plaintext SHA\n\tif err := downloadedHeader.VerifyPlaintextSHA(decrypted); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tcleanupCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t\tm.backend.Delete(cleanupCtx, m.bucket, key)\n\t}()\n\n\tresult.Status = StatusHealthy\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// checkMultipart performs a multipart canary check to exercise the multipart upload path.\nfunc (m *Monitor) checkMultipart(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.multipartSize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate multipart canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary-multipart/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload via multipart API (exercises create/upload/complete path)\n\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n\n\t// Step 1: Create multipart upload\n\tuploadID, err := m.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Upload parts (split into 2MB parts for reasonable part count)\n\tconst partSize = 2 * 1024 * 1024 // 2MB parts\n\tvar parts []backend.CompletedPart\n\tpartNum := int32(1)\n\n\tfor offset := 0; offset < len(envelope); offset += partSize {\n\t\tend := offset + partSize\n\t\tif end > len(envelope) {\n\t\t\tend = len(envelope)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartData := envelope[offset:end]\n\t\tpartETag, err := m.backend.UploadPart(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, partNum, bytes.NewReader(partData), int64(len(partData)))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// Abort on failure\n\t\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload part %d: %w\", partNum, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tparts = append(parts, backend.CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: partNum,\n\t\t\tETag:       partETag,\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tpartNum++\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 3: Complete multipart upload\n\t_, err = m.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Abort on failure\n\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to complete multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Download and verify (same verification as regular canary)\n\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download multipart canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tbody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded multipart canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\n\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\"CF-Cache-Status\"]; ok {\n\t\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \"HIT\" || cfStatus == \"STALE\" || cfStatus == \"REVALIDATED\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify size matches\n\tif len(downloadedEnvelope) != len(envelope) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"multipart download size mismatch: got %d, expected %d\", len(downloadedEnvelope), len(envelope))\n\t}\n\n\t// Byte-for-byte verification (critical for multipart integrity)\n\tif !bytes.Equal(downloadedEnvelope, envelope) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"multipart content byte-for-byte verification failed\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse header\n\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\n\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\n\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\n\n\tif hmacEnd > `json:\"multipart_last_check\"`\n\tMultipartConsecutiveFails  int       `json:\"multipart_consecutive_fails\"`\n\tMultipartLastError         string    `json:\"multipart_last_error,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Monitor manages the canary integrity checks.\ntype Monitor struct {\n\tbackend    backend.Backend\n\tbucket     string\n\tmek        []byte\n\tblockSize  int\n\tinstanceID string\n\n\tstate CanaryState\n\n\t// Configuration\n\tinterval          time.Duration\n\tcanarySize        int\n\tmaxRetries        int\n\tretryDelay        time.Duration\n\tmultipartInterval time.Duration\n\tmultipartSize     int\n\n\t// Control\n\tstopCh chan struct{}\n\tdoneCh chan struct{}\n}\n\n// Config holds configuration for the canary monitor.\ntype Config struct {\n\tBackend          backend.Backend\n\tBucket           string\n\tMEK              []byte\n\tBlockSize        int\n\tInstanceID       string\n\tInterval         time.Duration // Check interval (default 5 minutes)\n\tCanarySize       int           // Size of canary content (default 1024 bytes)\n\tMaxRetries       int           // Max retries on failure (default 3)\n\tRetryDelay       time.Duration // Delay between retries (default 10s)\n\tMultipartInterval time.Duration // Multipart check interval (default 1 hour)\n\tMultipartSize     int           // Size of multipart canary (default 6MB)\n}\n\n// NewMonitor creates a new canary monitor.\nfunc NewMonitor(cfg Config) *Monitor {\n\tif cfg.Interval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.Interval = 5 * time.Minute\n\t}\n\tif cfg.CanarySize == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.CanarySize = 1024\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MaxRetries == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MaxRetries = 3\n\t}\n\tif cfg.RetryDelay == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.RetryDelay = 10 * time.Second\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartInterval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartInterval = 1 * time.Hour\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartSize == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartSize = 6 * 1024 * 1024 // 6MB (above 5MB threshold)\n\t}\n\n\tinstanceID := cfg.InstanceID\n\tif instanceID == \"\" {\n\t\tinstanceID, _ = os.Hostname()\n\t\tif instanceID == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Generate random ID\n\t\t\tb := make([]byte, 8)\n\t\t\trand.Read(b)\n\t\t\tinstanceID = hex.EncodeToString(b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Monitor{\n\t\tbackend:           cfg.Backend,\n\t\tbucket:            cfg.Bucket,\n\t\tmek:               cfg.MEK,\n\t\tblockSize:         cfg.BlockSize,\n\t\tinstanceID:        instanceID,\n\t\tinterval:          cfg.Interval,\n\t\tcanarySize:        cfg.CanarySize,\n\t\tmaxRetries:        cfg.MaxRetries,\n\t\tretryDelay:        cfg.RetryDelay,\n\t\tmultipartInterval: cfg.MultipartInterval,\n\t\tmultipartSize:     cfg.MultipartSize,\n\t\tstopCh:            make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tdoneCh:            make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tstate: CanaryState{\n\t\t\tStatus:           StatusUnknown,\n\t\t\tMultipartHealthy: StatusUnknown,\n\t\t\tInstanceID:       instanceID,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// Start begins the periodic canary checks.\n// It runs an initial check immediately, then periodically.\nfunc (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) {\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer close(m.doneCh)\n\n\t\t// Initial checks\n\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\t\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\n\n\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)\n\t\tmultipartTicker := time.NewTicker(m.multipartInterval)\n\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\t\tdefer multipartTicker.Stop()\n\n\t\tfor {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-m.stopCh:\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\tcase <-multipartTicker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n}\n\n// Stop stops the canary monitor.\nfunc (m *Monitor) Stop() {\n\tclose(m.stopCh)\n\t<-m.doneCh\n}\n\n// runCheck performs a single canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tm.updateStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// runMultipartCheck performs a single multipart canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runMultipartCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.checkMultipart(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateMultipartStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(true)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(false)\n\tm.updateMultipartStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// check performs a single canary integrity check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) check(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.canarySize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload to B2 (direct, not through Cloudflare)\n\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n\tif err := m.backend.Put(ctx, m.bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Download through Cloudflare (via GetRangeWithHeaders to capture CF-Cache-Status)\n\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tbody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\n\t// CF-Cache-Status values: HIT, MISS, EXPIRED, STALE, BYPASS, REVALIDATED, UPDATING, IGNORED\n\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\"CF-Cache-Status\"]; ok {\n\t\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \"HIT\" || cfStatus == \"STALE\" || cfStatus == \"REVALIDATED\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse header\n\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\n\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\n\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\n\n\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\")\n\t}\n\n\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\n\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create decryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify HMACs\n\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\n\t\tresult.HMACVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.HMACVerified = true\n\n\t// Decrypt\n\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\n\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"decrypted content does not match original\")\n\t}\n\tresult.DecryptVerified = true\n\n\t// Verify plaintext SHA\n\tif err := downloadedHeader.VerifyPlaintextSHA(decrypted); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tcleanupCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t\tm.backend.Delete(cleanupCtx, m.bucket, key)\n\t}()\n\n\tresult.Status = StatusHealthy\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// checkMultipart performs a multipart canary check to exercise the multipart upload path.\nfunc (m *Monitor) checkMultipart(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.multipartSize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate multipart canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary-multipart/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: partNumber), nil\n262\t}\n263\t\n264\tfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n265\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n266\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n267\t\n268\t\t// Check if upload exists\n269\t\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n270\t\tif !exists {\n271\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n272\t\t}\n273\t\n274\t\t// Combine all parts in order\n275\t\tvar combined []byte\n276\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n277\t\t\tpartData, ok := key\n288\t\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n289\t\n290\t\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n291\t\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n292\t\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n293\t\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n294\t\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n295\t\t}\n296\t\n297\t\t// Clean up multipart data\n298\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n299\t\n300\t\t// Return final ETag\n301\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\tfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx {\n585\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n586\t\t}\n587\t\n588\t\t// Build envelope\n589\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n590\t\tenvelope = nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Read the part data\n\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read part data: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the part data\n\tif _, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]; !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tm.multipartData[uploadID][partNumber] = data\n\n\t// Return ETag (use hash of data for uniqueness)\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x-%d\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check if upload exists\n\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine all parts in order\n\tvar combined []byte\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the combined object\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n\n\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Return final ETag\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Clean up pending metadata\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) ListParts(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) (*backend.ListPartsResult, error) {\n\treturn &backend.ListPartsResult{}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) ListMultipartUploads(ctx TestMonitorGetStatus tests the status retrieval.\nfunc TestMonitorGetStatus(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend: 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\nfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\n\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n\n\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n\n\t// Create test content\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n\n\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\tif verifyErr == nil {\n\t\t\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\n\t\t\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\t\tif decryptErr == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\n\t\tt.Logf(\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\nfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tInterval:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Fast for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Start the monitor\n\tm.Start(ctx)\n\n\t// Wait for at least one check\n\ttime.Sleep(150 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Should be healthy now\n\tif !m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected monitor to be \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:            mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:      65536,\n\t\tInstanceID:     \"test-instance\",\n\t\tMultipartSize:  100, // Small size for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\n\t// Initial status: multipart_healthy should be false (never run)\n\tstatus := m.GetStatus()\n\tif status.MultipartHealthyBool {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected multipart_healthy to be false initially (never run)\")\n\t}\n\tif status.MultipartHealthy != StatusUnknown {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected multipart_healthy_status to be unknown initially, got %s\", status.MultipartHealthy)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run successful multipart check\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tresult, err := m.checkMultipart(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"multipart canary check failed: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Result should have multipart_healthy = true\n\tif !result.MultipartHealthyBool {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected multipart_healthy to be true in result after successful check\")\n\t}\n\tif result.MultipartHealthy != StatusHealthy {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected multipart_healthy_status to be healthy in result, got %s\", result.MultipartHealthy)\n\t}\n\n\t// Update the string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check if upload exists\n\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine all parts in order\n\tvar combined []byte\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the combined object\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n\n\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Return final ETag\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: TestMonitorGetStatus tests the status retrieval.\nfunc TestMonitorGetStatus(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\n\t// Initial status should be 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\nfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\n\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n\n\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n\n\t// Create test content\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n\n\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\tif verifyErr == nil {\n\t\t\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\n\t\t\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\t\tif decryptErr == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\n\t\tt.Logf(\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\nfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tInterval:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Fast for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Read the part data\n\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read part data: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the part data\n\tif _, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]; !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tm.multipartData[uploadID][partNumber] = data\n\n\t// Return ETag (use hash of data for uniqueness)\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x-%d\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check if upload exists\n\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine all parts in order\n\tvar combined []byte\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the combined object\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n\n\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Return final ETag\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Clean up pending metadata\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) TestMonitorGetStatus tests the status retrieval.\nfunc TestMonitorGetStatus(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend: tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\nfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\n\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n\n\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n\n\t// Create test content\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n\n\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\tif verifyErr == nil {\n\t\t\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\n\t\t\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\t\tif decryptErr == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\n\t\tt.Logf(\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\nfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tInterval:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Fast for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// headers\n\treturn io.NopCloser(bytes.NewReader(data[offset:end])), make(map[string]string), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) Head(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (*backend.ObjectInfo, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tk := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tdata, ok := m.objects[k]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"object not found: %s\", key)\n\t}\n\treturn &backend.ObjectInfo{\n\t\tKey:      key,\n\t\tSize:     int64(len(data)),\n\t\tMetadata: m.meta[k],\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) Delete(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tk := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tdelete(m.objects, k)\n\tdelete(m.meta, k)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) DeleteObjects(ctx context.Context, bucket string, keys []string) error {\n\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\tm.Delete(ctx, bucket, key)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) List(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*backend.ListResult, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tvar objects []backend.ObjectInfo\n\tprefix = bucket + \"/\" + prefix\n\tfor k, data := range m.objects {\n\t\tif len(prefix) > 0 && len(k) < len(prefix) || k[:len(prefix)] != prefix {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey := k[len(bucket)+1:]\n\t\tobjects = append(objects, backend.ObjectInfo{\n\t\t\tKey:      key,\n\t\t\tSize:     int64(len(data)),\n\t\t\tMetadata: m.meta[k],\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn &backend.ListResult{Objects: objects}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) Copy(ctx context.Context, srcBucket, srcKey, dstBucket, dstKey string, meta map[string]string, replaceMetadata bool) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tsrc := srcBucket + \"/\" + srcKey\n\tdst := dstBucket + \"/\" + dstKey\n\tdata, ok := m.objects[src]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"source object not found: %s\", srcKey)\n\t}\n\tm.objects[dst] = data\n\tif replaceMetadata {\n\t\tm.meta[dst] = meta\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Copy existing metadata\n\t\texistingMeta := m.meta[src]\n\t\tnewMeta := make(map[string]string)\n\t\tfor k, v := range existingMeta {\n\t\t\tnewMeta[k] = v\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor k, v := range meta {\n\t\t\tnewMeta[k] = v\n\t\t}\n\t\tm.meta[dst] = newMeta\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) ListBuckets(ctx context.Context) ([]backend.BucketInfo, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tbuckets := make(map[string]time.Time)\n\tfor k := range m.objects {\n\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(k, \"/\", 2)\n\t\tif len(parts) > 0 && parts[0] != \"\" {\n\t\t\tbucket := parts[0]\n\t\t\tif _, exists := buckets[bucket]; !exists {\n\t\t\t\tbuckets[bucket] = time.Now()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := make([]backend.BucketInfo, 0, len(buckets))\n\tfor name, created := range buckets {\n\t\tresult = append(result, backend.BucketInfo{\n\t\t\tName:         name,\n\t\t\tCreationDate: created,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CreateBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\tm.objects[bucket+\"/.bucket\"] = nil\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) DeleteBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\tfor k := range m.objects {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, bucket+\"/\") && k != bucket+\"/.bucket\" {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bucket not empty\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tdelete(m.objects, bucket+\"/.bucket\")\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) HeadBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\tfor k := range m.objects {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, bucket+\"/\") {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bucket not found: %s\", bucket)\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) GetDirect(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *backend.ObjectInfo, error) {\n\treturn m.Get(ctx, bucket, key)\n}\n\n// Multipart upload methods (functional implementations for testing)\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tuploadID := fmt.Sprintf(\"upload-%d\", time.Now().UnixNano())\n\t// Initialize storage for this upload's parts\n\tm.multipartData[uploadID] = make(map[int32][]byte)\n\t// Store metadata for later use when completing\n\tm.meta[bucket+\"/\"+key+\"__pending__\"+uploadID] = meta\n\treturn uploadID, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Read the part data\n\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read part data: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the part data\n\tif _, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]; !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tm.multipartData[uploadID][partNumber] = data\n\n\t// Return ETag (use hash of data for uniqueness)\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x-%d\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check if upload exists\n\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine all parts in order\n\tvar combined []byte\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the combined object\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n\n\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Return final ETag\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Clean up pending metadata\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) ListParts(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) (*backend.ListPartsResult, error) {\n\treturn &backend.ListPartsResult{}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) ListMultipartUploads(ctx context.Context, bucket string) (*backend.ListMultipartUploadsResult, error) {\n\treturn &backend.ListMultipartUploadsResult{}, nil\n}\n\n// Lifecycle configuration methods (stub implementations for testing)\nfunc (m *mockBackend) GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx context.Context, bucket string) ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"lifecycle configuration not found\")\n}\n\nfunc (m fmt.Errorf(\"not implemented\")\n}\n\n// TestNewMonitor tests Monitor creation.\nfunc TestNewMonitor(t *testing.T) {\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    newMockBackend(),\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tif m == nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"expected monitor, got nil\")\n\t}\n\tif m.bucket != \"test-bucket\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected bucket test-bucket, got %s\", m.bucket)\n\t}\n\tif m.instanceID != \"test-instance\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected instance ID test-instance, got %s\", m.instanceID)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestNewMonitorDefaults tests that default values are set correctly.\nfunc TestNewMonitorDefaults(t *testing.T) {\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:   newMockBackend(),\n\t\tBucket:    \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:       mek,\n\t\tBlockSize: 65536,\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tif m.interval != 5*time.Minute {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected default interval 5m, got %v\", m.interval)\n\t}\n\tif m.canarySize != 1024 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected default canary size 1024, got %d\", m.canarySize)\n\t}\n\tif m.maxRetries != 3 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected default max retries 3, got %d\", m.maxRetries)\n\t}\n\tif m.retryDelay != 10*time.Second {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected default retry delay 10s, got %v\", m.retryDelay)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorCheck tests a single canary check.\nfunc TestMonitorCheck(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tCanarySize: 100,\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"canary check failed: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif result.Status != StatusHealthy {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected status healthy, got %s\", result.Status)\n\t}\n\tif !result.DecryptVerified {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected decrypt verified to be true\")\n\t}\n\tif !result.HMACVerified {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected HMAC verified to be true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify canary was cleaned up (async, so wait a bit)\n\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\tmb.mu.Lock()\n\tcount := len(mb.objects)\n\tmb.mu.Unlock()\n\tif count > 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected canary to be cleaned up, found %d objects\", count)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorGetStatus tests the status retrieval.\nfunc TestMonitorGetStatus(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\n\t// Initial status should be unknown\n\tstatus := m.GetStatus()\n\tif status.Status != StatusUnknown {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected initial status unknown, got %s\", status.Status)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run a check\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\n\t// Status should now be healthy\n\tstatus = m.GetStatus()\n\tif status.Status != StatusHealthy {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected status healthy after check, got %s\", status.Status)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorIsHealthy tests the IsHealthy method.\nfunc TestMonitorIsHealthy(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\n\t// Initially unknown, not healthy\n\tif m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected IsHealthy to be false initially\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Run a check\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\n\t// Now should be healthy\n\tif !m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected IsHealthy to be true after successful check\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorWrongMEK tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\nfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\n\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n\n\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n\n\t// Create test content\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n\n\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\tif verifyErr == nil {\n\t\t\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\n\t\t\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\t\tif decryptErr == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\n\t\tt.Logf(\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\nfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tInterval:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Fast for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Start the monitor\n\tm.Start(ctx)\n\n\t// Wait for at least one check\n\ttime.Sleep(150 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Should be healthy now\n\tif !m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected monitor to be healthy after start\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Stop the monitor\n\tm.Stop()\n}\n\n// TestMarshalJSON tests JSON serialization.\nfunc TestMarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\n\tdata, err := m.MarshalJSON()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to marshal: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Should contain expected fields\n\ts := string(data)\n\tif !bytes.Contains([]byte(s), []byte(`\"status\"`)) {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected JSON to contain status field\")\n\t}\n\tif !bytes.Contains([]byte(s), []byte(`\"instance_id\"`)) {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected JSON to contain instance_id field\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestResultJSON tests Result JSON marshaling.\nfunc TestResultJSON(t *testing.T) {\n\tresult := Result{\n\t\tStatus:          StatusHealthy,\n\t\tLastCheck:       time.Now(),\n\t\tUploadLatencyMs:  45,\n\t\tDownloadLatencyMs: 12,\n\t\tDecryptVerified:  true,\n\t\tHMACVerified:     true,\n\t\tCFCacheHit:       false,\n\t}\n\n\t// Just verify it can be serialized\n\tdata, _ := hex.DecodeString(\"\") // placeholder - we just want the struct to be objects\", count)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorMultipartIntegration tests the full multipart check with metrics.\nfunc TestMonitorMultipartIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:        mb,\n\t\tBucket:         \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:            mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:      65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: _, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]; !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tm.multipartData[uploadID][partNumber] = data\n\n\t// Return ETag (use hash of data for uniqueness)\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x-%d\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check if upload exists\n\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine all parts in order\n\tvar combined []byte\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the combined object\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n\n\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Return final ETag\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Clean up pending metadata\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockBackend) tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\nfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\n\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n\n\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n\n\t// Create test content\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n\n\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\tif verifyErr == nil {\n\t\t\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\n\t\t\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\t\tif decryptErr == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\n\t\tt.Logf(\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\nfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tInterval:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Fast for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t//
